steps.title: Spot the contrast signal
steps.content: The word “instead” shows the romantic style does something different, indicating the realist style does the opposite of accentuating positive traits.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The blank describes what the realist style did to the romantic conventions — the word “instead” signals the realist style did the opposite of accentuating positive traits. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — epitomized: perfectly represented or embodied — this says realism was the best example of romantic conventions, which contradicts the contrast the passage draws between the two styles. ✘
  • Choice B — rejected: refused to accept or follow — realism turning away from romantic conventions explains why it did the opposite (emphasized accuracy rather than flattery). ✔
  • Choice C — imitated: copied or mimicked — this says realism followed romantic conventions, directly contradicting the contrast signaled by “instead.” ✘
  • Choice D — extended: built upon or continued — this says realism expanded romantic conventions, again contradicting the opposition the passage establishes. ✘
That is the correct answer.
